seed_problem: Pack polyominoes into a W x H board maximizing covered area (score = 1e5*sum(k)/(W*H)), reflections allowed; checker validates no-overlap and in-bounds.
mutation: composite: keep the 2D polyomino board-packing goal but attach a per-piece revenue and a per-type bounded inventory (turning area coverage into a 2D bounded-knapsack), and generalize orientations to the full 8-element dihedral group; the reference baseline is the best single-type regular tiling, so mixed-type gap-filling with rotations/reflections is the open-ended lever.
title: Carnival Ride Circuit: Fairground Footprint Packing
strategies: single-type regular tiling baseline: pick the ride type whose axis-aligned grid tiling yields the most revenue and stamp it out (the scored reference)
density-ordered one-pass greedy: install ride types in decreasing revenue-per-cell order, scanning the lot left-to-right with orientation r=0 only
fill-first-free-cell packing with all 8 orientations plus seeded multi-restart, keeping the highest-revenue layout found
mixed-type gap filling / local search that inserts small high-density footprints into the walkway gaps left by large rides
